rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Navistar, Inc'S Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 9, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Navistar, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is continued to March 21, 2017 pursuant to the March 8, 2017 stipulation and order. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA PONCHIONE VS. CONSOLIDATED INSULATION, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 9, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant CSK Auto, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' Third and Fourth causes of action: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter.

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD SAMPSON JR. VS. CBS CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, FKA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 9, 2017 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's general demurrer to plaintiff's complaint in its entirety is sustained on the ground that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (C.C.P. § 430.10(e).) On its face, the complaint demonstrates that plaintiff's exposure to asbestos allegedly attributable to defendant occurred during plaintiff's employment by PG&E, limiting plaintiff's available remedies to those under the Workers' Compensation Act. (Labor Code §§ 3600, 3602.) Plaintiff's complaint does not state facts sufficient to come within an exception to the workers' compensation exclusivity rule. Specifically, it does not state facts sufficient to state a claim for fraudulent concealment pursuant to Labor Code section 3602(b)(2). Plaintiff is granted 10 days' leave to amend to have an opportunity to cure these defects. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
